benefits:

The plant's leaves and bark are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other conditions. intro: Ozoroa concolor, commonly known as the white milk bush, is a shrub-like plant species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The white milk bush has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ozoroa concolor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, with the most common preparations being a tea made from the leaves and bark, and a topical cream for skin conditions.
